topics.name: Quadratic Equations
topics.objectives: Solve quadratic equations using multiple methods; analyse the nature of roots using the discriminant; solve quadratic inequalities and represent solutions on a number line.
topics.common_mistakes: Sign errors when completing the square; forgetting to reverse the inequality sign when multiplying/dividing by a negative; confusing 'no real roots' (discriminant < 0) with 'no roots'.
topics.remediation: Start with factorisation drills, then move to completing the square with guided steps. Use graphical approach to connect discriminant to the parabola's position relative to the x-axis.

topics.name: Algebraic Manipulation
topics.objectives: Manipulate algebraic expressions fluently; simplify and operate with algebraic fractions; decompose expressions into partial fractions; work with surds and index laws.
topics.common_mistakes: Incorrectly cancelling terms (instead of factors) in fractions; distributing exponents over sums; rationalising the denominator with wrong conjugate.
topics.remediation: Drill factor vs. term distinction with visual examples. Practice index laws systematically before combining with surds.

topics.name: Indices and Surds
topics.objectives: Apply index laws to simplify expressions; convert between surd and index form; rationalise denominators; solve equations involving indices and surds.
topics.common_mistakes: Confusing multiplication and addition rules for indices (a^m * a^n vs a^m + a^n); forgetting to square both sides carefully when solving surd equations (introducing extraneous roots).
topics.remediation: Build index law fluency with pattern recognition exercises. For surds, emphasise checking for extraneous solutions after squaring.

topics.name: Polynomials
topics.objectives: Use the remainder and factor theorems to factorise polynomials; solve cubic equations; sketch polynomial graphs showing key features.
topics.common_mistakes: Sign errors in synthetic division; forgetting that Factor Theorem gives (x - a) when f(a) = 0 (not (x + a)); incomplete factorisation of cubics.
topics.remediation: Practice synthetic division step-by-step. Use systematic trial of integer factors combined with Factor Theorem before attempting full factorisation.

topics.name: Simultaneous Equations
topics.objectives: Solve simultaneous equations involving one linear and one non-linear equation; interpret solutions graphically as intersection points.
topics.common_mistakes: Substitution errors when expression is complex; forgetting to find both variables after solving for one; not checking solutions in both original equations.
topics.remediation: Always substitute back to verify. Start with simpler linear-linear systems before progressing to linear-quadratic pairs.

topics.name: Logarithms and Exponentials
subtopics: Graphs of exponential and log functions
topics.objectives: Convert between exponential and logarithmic forms; apply log laws to simplify expressions; solve exponential and logarithmic equations; sketch and interpret graphs.
topics.common_mistakes: Applying log laws incorrectly: log(a+b) ≠ log(a) + log(b); forgetting domain restrictions (argument of log must be positive); errors with change of base.
topics.remediation: Emphasise that log laws mirror index laws. Use numerical examples to build intuition before algebraic manipulation. Always check domain validity of solutions.

topics.name: Coordinate Geometry
topics.objectives: Find distances, midpoints, and gradients; determine equations of lines given various conditions; apply parallel/perpendicular gradient relationships; calculate areas using the Shoelace formula.
topics.common_mistakes: Sign errors in gradient calculation (y2-y1)/(x2-x1); confusing perpendicular gradient (negative reciprocal) with parallel (same gradient); errors in Shoelace formula ordering.
topics.remediation: Drill gradient calculations with varied point orderings. Visualise on graph paper before calculating. Practice Shoelace with consistent anticlockwise vertex ordering.

topics.name: Linear Law
subtopics: Reducing non-linear relations to linear form
topics.objectives: Transform non-linear equations into Y = mX + c form; plot data in transformed coordinates; extract original equation parameters from gradient and intercept.
topics.common_mistakes: Choosing the wrong transformation (e.g. plotting ln(y) vs x when the relationship is y = ax^n, which needs ln(y) vs ln(x)); reading gradient from incorrectly scaled axes.
topics.remediation: Create a reference table of common non-linear forms and their transformations. Practice identifying which transformation to use before plotting.

topics.name: Trigonometric Functions
topics.objectives: Evaluate trigonometric ratios for any angle; sketch and transform trig graphs; determine amplitude, period, and phase shift; solve basic trig equations in given intervals.
topics.common_mistakes: Calculator in wrong mode (degrees vs radians); not finding all solutions in a given interval; errors with ASTC rule for signs in different quadrants.
topics.remediation: Always verify calculator mode first. Use the unit circle and ASTC diagram systematically. Practice finding general solutions before restricting to intervals.

topics.name: Trigonometric Identities
subtopics: R-formula (a sin θ + b cos θ)
topics.objectives: Prove and apply trigonometric identities; use addition and double angle formulas; express a sin θ + b cos θ in the form R sin(θ ± α); solve trigonometric equations using identities.
topics.common_mistakes: Misremembering double-angle formulas; errors in R-formula (wrong quadrant for α); attempting to prove identities by manipulating both sides simultaneously instead of one side only.
topics.remediation: Derive double-angle formulas from addition formulas each time to build understanding rather than memorising. Practice R-formula with a clear step-by-step template.

topics.name: Differentiation
subtopics: Derivatives of trig, exponential, and log functions
topics.objectives: Differentiate standard functions using power, chain, product, and quotient rules; find derivatives of composite, trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions.
topics.common_mistakes: Forgetting the chain rule on composite functions; sign error in quotient rule (denominator order); not simplifying before differentiating (missing easier approaches).
topics.remediation: Practice identifying which rule to apply before computing. Use colour-coding to identify inner and outer functions for chain rule. Simplify expressions algebraically first when possible.

topics.name: Applications of Differentiation
topics.objectives: Find equations of tangents and normals; determine stationary points and their nature; solve optimisation problems; apply connected rates of change using the chain rule.
topics.common_mistakes: Using gradient of tangent for normal (forgetting negative reciprocal); not verifying nature of stationary point (second derivative or sign test); setting up wrong equation in optimisation word problems.
topics.remediation: For optimisation: practice translating word problems into equations with a single variable before differentiating. Always verify the nature of critical points.

topics.name: Integration
topics.objectives: Integrate standard functions; evaluate definite integrals; find areas bounded by curves, lines, and axes; apply integration to kinematic problems.
topics.common_mistakes: Forgetting +C in indefinite integrals; sign errors when area is below x-axis (must take absolute value); incorrect limits of integration; forgetting to divide by the coefficient of x in (ax+b)^n.
topics.remediation: Always check by differentiating the result. For area problems, sketch the curve first and identify regions above and below the axis separately.

topics.name: Binomial Theorem
subtopics: Binomial expansion for positive integer index
topics.objectives: Expand (a + b)^n using the Binomial Theorem; find specific terms including the general term T(r+1); use substitution to find approximations.
topics.common_mistakes: Off-by-one errors with the general term index (T_{r+1} not T_r); forgetting to apply the power to both parts of the term; sign errors when b is negative.
topics.remediation: Write out the general term formula explicitly each time. Practice with negative and fractional terms inside the binomial to build confidence with signs.

topics.name: Sets and Venn Diagrams
subtopics: Venn diagrams with two and three sets
topics.objectives: Use set notation correctly; represent set relationships with Venn diagrams; solve problems involving union, intersection, and complement of sets.
topics.common_mistakes: Double-counting elements in overlapping regions; confusing A' (complement) with A; forgetting the universal set when computing complements.
topics.remediation: Always label all regions of the Venn diagram including the region outside all sets (but inside the universal set). Work from the innermost overlap outward.

topics.name: Permutations and Combinations
topics.objectives: Apply counting principles to solve arrangement and selection problems; distinguish between permutations and combinations; handle restrictions and repeated elements.
topics.common_mistakes: Using permutations when order doesn't matter (and vice versa); forgetting to subtract restricted cases; not accounting for identical items in arrangements.
topics.remediation: Ask 'Does order matter?' as the first step every time. Use complementary counting (total minus restricted) for problems with exclusions. Draw slot diagrams for arrangements.

topics.name: Probability
topics.objectives: Calculate probabilities using addition and multiplication rules; distinguish between mutually exclusive and independent events; solve conditional probability problems; use tree diagrams systematically.
topics.common_mistakes: Adding probabilities when events are not mutually exclusive (forgetting to subtract the intersection); confusing independent and mutually exclusive; errors in conditional probability (wrong denominator).
topics.remediation: Always draw a tree diagram or two-way table for multi-stage problems. Clearly identify whether events are independent or dependent before choosing a formula.

topics.name: Proofs in Plane Geometry
subtopics: Symmetric and angle properties of circles
topics.objectives: Apply circle theorems to find unknown angles; prove geometric properties using logical chains of reasoning; construct clear geometric proofs.
topics.common_mistakes: Citing wrong theorems; incomplete proof chains (jumping steps); confusing 'angle at centre' with 'angle in same segment' theorems.
topics.remediation: Create a theorem reference card with diagrams. Practice identifying which theorem applies by the geometric configuration before attempting the proof.
